title: Store Food Properly
summary: Put leftovers and ingredients in the right containers at the right temperature so they stay fresh and safe.
learnerPromise: Store leftovers and ingredients so they stay fresh longer.
whyItMatters: Good storage keeps food safe to eat, reduces waste, and saves money. A well-organized fridge also makes it easier to see what you have and plan meals around it.
realLifeUses: Keep leftovers safe to eat for several days
Organize the fridge so older food gets used first
Freeze extra portions before they go bad
Know which foods go in the fridge, freezer, or pantry
youWillLearn: How to choose airtight containers for different foods
Where to place items in the fridge for best temperature control
How to label containers with dates
The difference between fridge storage and freezer storage
miniChallenge: Move one leftover into an airtight container and label it with today's date.
steps: Let hot leftovers cool on the counter for about 20 minutes before refrigerating.
Choose a clean, airtight container that fits the portion without too much empty space.
Transfer the food into the container and seal the lid tightly.
Write today's date on a piece of tape or a sticky note and attach it to the container.
Place the container in the fridge where you can see it, ideally on a middle shelf.
completionCriteria: Leftover is sealed in an airtight container
Container is clearly labeled with today's date
commonProblems: Container is not truly airtight — check that the lid clicks or seals fully
Forgot what day the food was stored — labeling solves this
Fridge is too crowded — move older items to the front so they get used first
tips: Use clear containers so you can see what is inside without opening them
Store raw meat on the bottom shelf to prevent drips onto other foods
Keep the fridge at or below 40 degrees Fahrenheit for safe storage
